judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable substance; Report 2 states the RESULTS_DB collision risk slightly more directly and separates checkout isolation from DB isolation more explicitly, and also catches the pre-existing failing test test_full_credit as an added coordination risk, but the core expectations are met equivalently by both.

report_1.evidence: Workstream C — pipeline/registry.py: F-05, F-09, and F-02 (once unblocked). Three one-line config edits in one 11-line file — trivially one engineer.

report_2.evidence: Batch C — registry config (Engineer 3): F-02 (add aurora-1 endpoint), F-05 (default rubric version → v3), F-09 (dataset path → fixtures/eval_set.jsonl), all in pipeline/registry.py.

report_1.evidence: Ordering across streams: A–D can all start simultaneously — they touch disjoint files, which is the point of the grouping. ... Workstream C — pipeline/registry.py: F-05, F-09, and F-02 (once unblocked). Three one-line config edits in one 11-line file — trivially one engineer.

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (fully parallel): Batches A–D simultaneously. ... A owns rubric.py, B owns run_batch.py, C owns registry.py, D owns prompts/. ... Batch C — registry config (Engineer 3): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ..., all in pipeline/registry.py.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — needs a decision, not an implementation. ... The current values (0.35, 0.65) at score.py:3 are a scoring-policy knob: changing them silently shifts every score and breaks comparability with previously saved results. An engineer can't pick the new numbers from anything in this repo — the eval owner has to choose them

report_2.evidence: F-06 (tighten PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS in pipeline/score.py). "Too lenient; tighten them" specifies no target values, and this changes user-facing scoring outcomes for borderline answers. That's a product/calibration decision, not an engineering fix — I'd escalate it for someone who owns scoring policy to pick the numbers, and only then schedule the implementation (Wave 2).

report_1.evidence: A private RESULTS_DB. tests/conftest.py:4 reads RESULTS_DB with a machine-global default of /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db; each engineer should export a per-user path to avoid cross-engineer collisions on shared machines.

report_2.evidence: A shared mutable test resource. tests/conftest.py:4 points the suite at RESULTS_DB defaulting to /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db — a machine-global path. Parallel test runs from multiple engineers on one machine would stomp each other's results database.
